reforms.description: The Belgian government has introduced green certificates to encourage the production of electricity from renewable sources, compensating producers for the higher costs associated with renewable energy. Additionally, the federal government provides tax deductions for households and enterprises investing in renewable energy, allowing households to deduct up to 40% and enterprises 13.5% from taxable profits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to stimulate investment in renewable energy, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Both the green certificates and tax deductions are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at promoting renewable energy investment.
reforms.source_quote: At the federal level, households can deduct up to 40% of their investment in renewable energy from income tax. Enterprises can deduct 13.5% of their investment in renewable energy from the taxable profits.
